author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the Voice of the Mediator Himself, strong of hand in the conflict of the Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now what He seems to wish for against His enemies, is not the wish of malevolence, but the declaration of their punishment; as in the Gospel, with the cities, in which though He had performed miracles, yet they had not believed on Him, He doth not wish in any evil will what He saith, but predicteth what is impending over them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto Thee, O Lord, have I cried; My God, be not silent from me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto Thee, O Lord, have I cried; My God, separate not the unity of Thy Word from that which as Man I am.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lest at any time Thou be silent from me: and I shall be like them that go down into the pit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from this, that the Eternity of Thy Word ceaseth not to unite Itself to Me, it comes that I am not such a man as the rest of men, who are born into the deep misery of this world: where, as if Thou art silent, Thy Word is not recognised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear, O Lord, the voice of my supplication, whist I pray unto Thee, whilst I hold up my hands to Thy holy temple” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whilst I am crucified for their salvation, who on believing become Thy holy temple.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Draw not My Soul away with sinners, and destroy me not with them that work iniquity, with them that speak peace with their neighbours” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With them that say unto Me, “We know that Thou art a Master come from God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But evil in their hearts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they speak evil in their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give unto them according to their works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give unto them according to their works, for this is just.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And according to the malice of their affections.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For aiming at evil, they cannot discover good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “According to the works of their hands give Thou unto them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although what they have done may avail for salvation to others, yet give Thou unto them according to the works of their wills.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Pay them their recompense.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, for the truth which they heard, they wished to recompense deceit; let their own deceit deceive them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they have not had understanding in the works of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence is it clear that this hath befallen them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From this forsooth, “for they have not had understanding in the works of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This very thing, in truth, hath been, even now, their recompense, that in Him whom they tempted with malicious intent as a Man, they should not recognise God, with what design the Father sent Him in the Flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the works of His hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor be moved by those visible works, which are laid out before their very eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt destroy them, and not build them up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them do Me no hurt, nay, nor again in their endeavour to raise engines against My Church, let them aught avail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed be the Lord, for He hath heard the voice of My prayer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord My Helper and My Protector” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord helping Me in so great sufferings, and protecting Me with immortality in My resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Him hath My Heart trusted, and I have been helped.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My Flesh hath flourished again:” that is, and My Flesh hath risen again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And of my will I will confess unto Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, the fear of death being now destroyed, not by the necessity of fear under the Law, but with a free will with the Law, shall they who believe on Me, confess unto Him; and because I am in them, I will confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is the strength of His people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that people “ignorant of the righteousness of God, and willing to establish their own.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they thought not themselves strong in themselves: for the Lord is the strength of His people, struggling in this life’s difficulties with the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the protector of the salvation of His Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That, having saved them by His Christ after the strength of war, He may protect them at the last with the immortality of peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save Thy people, and bless Thine inheritance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I intercede therefore, after My Flesh hath flourished again, because Thou hast said, “Desire of Me, and I will give Thee the heathen for Thine inheritance;” “Save Thy people, and bless Thine inheritance:” for “all Mine are Thine.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And rule them, and set them up even for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And rule them in this temporal life, and raise them from hence into life eternal.
